text: Watch a pendulum. It swings. And swings. And after a while — it stops.
visual: Side view. A simple pendulum swinging on a string from a fixed point. Three full swings shown, then amplitude visibly decreases until it comes to rest at the bottom.

text: Natural question: where did its energy go?
visual: The pendulum hangs still at the bottom. A question mark fades in beside it.

text: Most people answer in two words. It ran out.
visual: The phrase 'it ran out' appears in plain typography in the center of the frame.

text: That answer is wrong. Energy doesn't run out. Ever.
visual: A red line strikes through 'it ran out.' Then it fades.

text: To see why, leave the pendulum for a second. Open your wallet.
visual: The pendulum scene fades out. A wallet fades in on a wooden table, viewed from a slight overhead angle.

text: Yesterday you had fifty riyals. Today you have forty.
visual: The wallet opens. Split frame: 'YESTERDAY' label with a stack of bills marked '50', 'TODAY' label with a smaller stack marked '40'.

text: Did the ten riyals disappear?
visual: A red question mark hovers over a faded outline of a missing 10-riyal bill between the two stacks.

text: No. Nobody assumes money disappears. You start looking for the receipt.
visual: Cut to a person's hands rummaging in pockets. Small paper receipts begin to spill onto the table one by one.

text: Maybe coffee. Maybe parking. Maybe some small thing you forgot. But it went somewhere.
visual: Three receipts on the table light up in turn, each with a small icon: a coffee cup, a parking sign, a generic shop bag.

text: That's the rule. Money doesn't vanish. It moves.
visual: Text fades in beneath the receipts: 'Money doesn't vanish. It moves.'

text: Now go back to the pendulum. Same reflex.
visual: Smooth transition back to the pendulum, now still at the bottom.

text: Its energy didn't run out. It was spent. We just have to find the receipt.
visual: Beside the pendulum, 'ran out' fades out and 'spent' fades in to replace it. A small receipt icon hovers in the corner.

text: But spent on what? The pendulum doesn't drink coffee.
visual: A small thought bubble next to the pendulum shows a coffee cup with a red strike-through.

text: Look closer. Every swing, the pendulum pushes air.
visual: Camera zooms in toward the pendulum's path. Faint curved motion lines appear, indicating air being pushed out of the way.

text: And air isn't nothing. Air is billions of tiny molecules, sitting in its way.
visual: Zoom continues until tiny dots — air molecules — become visible, drifting slowly throughout the empty space around the pendulum.

text: It bumps them. They start moving faster.
visual: The pendulum swings through the frame. The molecules nearest its path visibly speed up — their motion becoming fast and jittery.

text: And moving faster has a name. We call it heat.
visual: The fast-moving molecules glow with a soft warm tint. A label fades in beside them: 'heat.'

text: Every swing — a small piece of swing-energy becomes a small piece of heat.
visual: Split frame. Left: the pendulum with a vertical bar labeled 'swing energy' that shrinks slightly. Right: the surrounding air with a vertical bar labeled 'heat' that grows by the same amount.

text: Pay. Pay. Pay. Millions of swings, millions of tiny payments.
visual: Time-lapse. The pendulum swings rapidly. With each swing, a tiny coin-like token visibly transfers from the pendulum into the surrounding air.

text: Until the wallet is empty. And the pendulum stops.
visual: The pendulum's swing decays to zero. It hangs still at the bottom. The 'swing energy' bar from earlier is now empty.

text: But the room — the room is slightly warmer. By an amount you can't feel.
visual: Camera pulls back to reveal the whole room. The air carries a very faint warm tint. A small thermometer in the corner has risen by an imperceptible amount.

text: That's the receipt.
visual: A small receipt icon fades in beside the still pendulum, labeled in faint text: 'air, slightly warmer.'

text: Physics has an equation for this. Total energy before = total energy after.
visual: An equation fades in elegantly: KE + PE = constant. Below it in smaller text: 'energy before = energy after.' Glows briefly.

text: It tells you the total never changes. It doesn't tell you where to look.
visual: The equation dims and slides to a corner of the frame, leaving the still pendulum at center.

text: The habit is what matters. Don't ask where the energy went.
visual: The question 'where did the energy go?' appears, then a line strikes through it.

text: Ask: what got warmer? What got louder? What moved?
visual: Three icons fade in one by one to a steady beat: a thermometer (warmth), a small speaker (sound), an arrow (motion).

text: Car brakes get hot. A bouncing ball thuds quieter and quieter as the floor warms. Your phone 'dies' — its heat is in your hand.
visual: Three quick cuts: glowing red-hot brake discs of a stopping car; a bouncing ball with faint heat glow and small sound waves on the floor; a phone in a hand with a soft warm aura.

text: Nothing ran out. Everything was spent.
visual: The three example icons appear briefly side-by-side, each with a small receipt icon beneath it. Then dissolve.

text: Back to the pendulum. The last swing. The air around it is carrying everything it had — a warmth you couldn't see.
visual: Callback to the opening shot. The pendulum completes one final swing and comes to rest. This time, the air around it visibly pulses with a soft warm glow that radiates outward into the room before fading.
